text: The gcd function implementation is incorrect because it fails to swap the arguments in the recursive call, causing infinite recursion and eventually a RecursionError. Specifically, it always recurses with gcd(a % b, b) instead of gcd(b, a % b), which is the correct Euclidean algorithm step. This causes the function not to progress towards the base case when b != 0, leading to all tests failing except possibly trivial ones.

data.label: The gcd function causes infinite recursion and fails tests
Incorrect argument order in recursive call gcd(a % b, b) leads to non-termination
Correct gcd uses gcd(b, a % b) in recursion
